title: pimpinella trichomanifolia
intro: Pimpinella trichomanifolia, also known as Shegal, is an herbaceous plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae), native to the Himalayan region. The medicinal properties of Pimpinella trichomanifolia lie in its seeds and roots, which contain compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of this plant include its use in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and as an antiseptic to heal wounds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate menstrual discomfort. The herbal preparations of Pimpinella trichomanifolia include its seeds being used in traditional medicine, often in combination with other herbs, and its roots being made into a decoction for therapeutic purposes.
